title: About me
text:
E-mail
  
  
    
     LinkedIn
  

  
  
I am a UKRI-funded PhD candidate studying plant functional ecology in dry tropical ecosystems, with a background in biology and applied data science.
Growing up in Colombia, I found my passion for becoming a scientist and speaking for ecosystems that cannot speak for themselves. I became a biologist at UNAL where I learned about functional traits and ecological restoration. I developed my interest in ecosystem modelling through an MSc in data science at UoEx. I value reproducibility, collaboration and innovation. I try to put these values into my functional trait research about savannas, contrasting water and fire strategies across Africa and South America.
My broad interests include plant functional ecology and data science, with a focus on plant organs such as roots and hydraulic traits. I am interested in modelling ecosystems and aid ecosystem restoration and management for sustainable futures. I value the great biodiversity of tropical ecosystems, and I am keen on broadening understanding of these regions and their services for local communities and society.

title: Presenting my PhD proposal
text:
As part of the doctoral program, a report is presented after 10 months to show progress and structured plan for the whole PhD project. I presented my ideas to experienced and knowledgeable examiners as well as to collegueas from the Gw4+ program at the thematic meeting Environmental solutions at the National History Museum (NHM) in London and the Geography department at Univeristy of Exeter along with other colleagues.



Presentation at the NHM in London





Upgrade presentation at Physics Building, UoE
